examples.description: right lower leg flap reconstruction follow-up — full note: structured history, medications, labelled wound and flap findings, and an assessment + plan synthesized and cited from the captured findings (Grounded Synthesis Mode, v3.2)

transcript.text: What's bringing you back in today?
I'm here for the wound on my right lower leg. It's been draining and the dressing keeps soaking through.
The wound started after my car accident three weeks ago. You did the flap reconstruction two weeks ago and it's been seeping more over the last few days.
I have type two diabetes, and I smoke about half a pack a day.
I'm taking metformin 500 milligrams twice daily and cefalexin 500 milligrams four times a day, plus Tylenol as needed for pain.
Any allergies? Yes, penicillin gives me a rash.
Let's take the dressing down and have a look at the right lower leg wound.
The wound measures six by three by one centimetre over the anterior right lower leg.
The wound bed is about seventy percent granulation and thirty percent slough, with no eschar.
There's moderate serosanguineous exudate, no purulence and no odour today.
There's about half a centimetre of undermining at the twelve o'clock position. The wound edges are approximated elsewhere.
Periwound skin shows mild erythema extending roughly one centimetre, with some induration but no maceration.
The flap itself is pink with a good colour match, capillary refill is brisk at about two seconds, and it's warm with no congestion or pallor.
On the X-ray of the right tibia there's no cortical lucency or periosteal reaction at the wound site.
I'll send a wound swab for culture and order a CBC and CRP today.
I'll refer you to the wound care nurse for dressing changes, and to smoking cessation.

We can switch to a silver foam dressing, about thirty dollars per change, which helps manage exudate and bacterial load; the trade-off is the cost over standard foam. Keep the leg elevated and avoid weight-bearing for now, and come back in three days.

claims.text: Draining wound of the right lower leg with dressing strikethrough.
claims.source_quote: I'm here for the wound on my right lower leg. It's been draining and the dressing keeps soaking through.

claims.text: Wound originated after a motor vehicle accident three weeks ago; flap reconstruction performed two weeks ago, with increased seepage over the last few days.
claims.source_quote: The wound started after my car accident three weeks ago. You did the flap reconstruction two weeks ago and it's been seeping more over the last few days.

claims.text: Type 2 diabetes; current smoker, approximately half a pack per day.
claims.source_quote: I have type two diabetes, and I smoke about half a pack a day.

claims.text: Metformin 500 mg twice daily; cefalexin 500 mg four times daily; Tylenol as needed for pain.
claims.source_quote: I'm taking metformin 500 milligrams twice daily and cefalexin 500 milligrams four times a day, plus Tylenol as needed for pain.
Yes, penicillin gives me a rash.

claims.text: Location and dimensions: anterior right lower leg wound measuring 6 x 3 x 1 cm.
claims.source_quote: The wound measures six by three by one centimetre over the anterior right lower leg.

claims.text: Wound bed: approximately 70% granulation and 30% slough, no eschar.
claims.source_quote: The wound bed is about seventy percent granulation and thirty percent slough, with no eschar.

claims.text: Exudate: moderate serosanguineous exudate, no purulence and no odour.
claims.source_quote: There's moderate serosanguineous exudate, no purulence and no odour today.

claims.text: Wound edges: approximately 0.5 cm of undermining at the 12 o'clock position; edges otherwise approximated.
claims.source_quote: There's about half a centimetre of undermining at the twelve o'clock position. The wound edges are approximated elsewhere.

claims.text: Periwound skin: mild erythema extending approximately 1 cm with some induration, no maceration.
claims.source_quote: Periwound skin shows mild erythema extending roughly one centimetre, with some induration but no maceration.

claims.text: Flap viability: pink with good colour match, brisk capillary refill at approximately 2 seconds, warm, no congestion or pallor.
claims.source_quote: The flap itself is pink with a good colour match, capillary refill is brisk at about two seconds, and it's warm with no congestion or pallor.

claims.text: X-ray of the right tibia: no cortical lucency or periosteal reaction at the wound site.
claims.source_quote: On the X-ray of the right tibia there's no cortical lucency or periosteal reaction at the wound site.

claims.text: Working assessment: predominantly granulating anterior right lower-leg wound (6 x 3 x 1 cm) with moderate serosanguineous exudate and localised periwound erythema and induration, with 0.5 cm of undermining at 12 o'clock.
claims.source_quote: The wound bed is about seventy percent granulation and thirty percent slough, with no eschar.

Periwound skin shows mild erythema extending roughly one centimetre, with some induration but no maceration.
There's about half a centimetre of undermining at the twelve o'clock position. The wound edges are approximated elsewhere.

claims.text: Working assessment: viable flap — pink with good colour match, brisk capillary refill, warm, no congestion or pallor.
claims.source_quote: The flap itself is pink with a good colour match, capillary refill is brisk at about two seconds, and it's warm with no congestion or pallor.

claims.text: Investigations: wound swab for culture; CBC and CRP today.
claims.source_quote: I'll send a wound swab for culture and order a CBC and CRP today.

claims.text: Referrals: wound care nurse for dressing changes; smoking cessation.
claims.source_quote: I'll refer you to the wound care nurse for dressing changes, and to smoking cessation.

claims.text: Options discussed: switch to silver foam dressing (~$30 per change) to manage exudate and bacterial load; trade-off is added cost over standard foam.
claims.source_quote: We can switch to a silver foam dressing, about thirty dollars per change, which helps manage exudate and bacterial load; the trade-off is the cost over standard foam.

claims.text: Activity and follow-up: elevate the leg, avoid weight-bearing for now, and return in three days.
claims.source_quote: Keep the leg elevated and avoid weight-bearing for now, and come back in three days.
